reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all components of the informal statement: n ≥ 3 matches k ≥ 3; x, y : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) represents ℝ^k; ‖x - y‖ = d with d > 0 matches |x - y| = d > 0; r > 0 and 2r > d match the premises; and Set.Infinite {z | ‖z-x‖ = r ∧ ‖z-y‖ = r} precisely encodes "infinitely many z with |z-x| = |z-y| = r". The outer `z : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)` hypothesis is an unused variable that gets shadowed by the bound variable in the set-builder notation; since EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) is always nonempty, this redundant binding doesn't alter the logical strength. The statement is a strictly faithful formalization.
